content: Template talk:HTML - Wikipedia Jump to content From Wikipedia, the free encyclopedia "HTML editor" compared to "Editor (HTML)"[edit] Part of this series might be to rename all the articles... html series HTML Dynamic HTML Editor (HTML) Element (HTML) Font family (HTML) Scripting (HTML) Character encodings in HTML Unicode and HTML Comparison of layout engines (HTML) List of HTML editors Makes more sense to me than, html series HTML HTML editor Dynamic HTML HTML element List of HTML editors Character encodings in HTML Unicode and HTML Font family (HTML) HTML scripting Comparison of layout engines (HTML) —Sean κ. ⇔ 23:57, 20 May 2005 (UTC)[reply] YUCK putting a word in brackets after an article name is something that really should only be done when there is no proper unambiguous name for the article.
